article_title: Eli Lilly's Retatrutide Cuts Body Weight 28.3% in Phase 3 TRIUMPH-1 as Triple-Agonist Data Land at ADA 2026

content_preview.title: Eli Lilly's Retatrutide Cuts Body Weight 28.3% in Phase 3 TRIUMPH-1 as Triple-Agonist Data Land at ADA 2026
content_preview.summary: Eli Lilly presented phase 3 results for retatrutide at ADA 2026 on June 6, showing 28.3% mean weight loss at 80 weeks in obesity and a companion diabetes trial published in The Lancet.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Eli Lilly presented detailed phase 3 results for retatrutide, an experimental drug that activates three gut and metabolic hormone receptors at once, at the American Diabetes Association's Scientific Sessions on Saturday, June 6, 2026, at the Ernest N. Morial Convention Center in New Orleans, [according to BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/breakthrough-studies-demonstrate-effectiveness-of-the-first-triple-hormone-therapy-for-type-2-diabetes-and-obesity). In the pivota...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (913 words) using the Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis format. Technical clinical detail (dose-response, A1C thresholds, AHI, WOMAC) is presented accurately and accessibly. Every numeric claim carries an inline [according to ...] attribution to a specific source.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (none failed; status 200, no archive fallback). source-1.html.gz (PR Newswire, May 21 obesity topline): confirmed verbatim — TRIUMPH-1, 2,339 participants, 1:1:1:1, the full dose-response (4 mg 47.2 lbs/19.0%; 9 mg 64.4 lbs/25.9%; 12 mg 70.3 lbs/28.3%; placebo 5.5 lbs/2.2%), the 104-week BMI>=35 extension (85.0 lbs/30.3%), 45.3% achieving >=30% weight loss ('a level long associated with bariatric surgery'), 65.3% reaching BMI<30, the 2 mg step-wise dose-escalation quote, all 12 mg AEs (nausea 42.4 vs 14.8, diarrhea 32.0 vs 13.5, constipation 26.1 vs 10.9, vomiting 25.3 vs 4.8), 11.3% vs 4.9% discontinuation, and the Jastreboff lead-investigator quote with correct Yale title. source-0.html.gz (PR Newswire, June 6 ADA release): confirmed verbatim — A1C up to 2.0% from baseline 7.9%, up to 90% below 7.0%, up to 46% below 5.7% (normoglycemia), diabetes 12 mg weight loss 36.6 lbs/16.8% at 40 weeks, WOMAC knee-pain up to 4.3 points/73.1% from baseline 6.0, AHI up to 36.1 events/hr/60.6% from baseline 58.6, and the second Jastreboff '200 downstream diseases' quote. source-2.html.gz (BioSpace): confirmed verbatim — June 6 / Ernest N. Morial Convention Center / New Orleans, 537 participants over 40 weeks, simultaneous Lancet publication, 'first triple-hormone therapy', the safety statement ('similar to those of GLP-1 receptor agonists already in use, with no new reported safety concerns'), and the Bajaj quote attributed to 'Harpreet Singh Bajaj, MD, MPH, lead author of the study'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabricated or misattributed clinical statistic found. Rule 9 (clinical scrutiny) satisfied: every clinical specific traces to an accessible snapshot. The TRANSCEND-T2D-1 Lancet paper is referenced ONLY as a fact-of-publication, sourced to BioSpace/Lilly — no specific is drawn from the (inaccessible) Lancet DOI, which is not in the source array. The diabetes complications figures (A1C, WOMAC, AHI) are correctly cited to the June 6 Lilly release (source-0), not the Lancet. Both Jastreboff quotes are attributed to Jastreboff (source-0/source-1) and the Bajaj quote to Bajaj (source-2) — no speaker cross-attribution. No tirzepatide head-to-head comparison or '532'-style alternate cohort figure appears in the body.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Promotional framings ('remarkable potential', bariatric-surgery comparisons) are explicitly attributed to Lilly rather than asserted in the Herald's voice. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags the absence of a regulatory timeline, the lack of head-to-head data (cross-trial figures only), and uncertainty about the glucagon mechanism's long-term durability. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald coverage of retatrutide or its triple-agonist data. Adjacent obesity/GLP-1 articles (oral Foundayo, semaglutide trials, Roche amylin, orforglipron) cover different drugs/events and do not overlap with the TRIUMPH-1 / TRANSCEND-T2D-1 ADA 2026 results.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully sourced clinical News submission. Signature and payload hash valid; bidirectional source coverage intact (3 body URLs == 3 source-array URLs, no orphans). Every clinical number, dose, adverse-event rate, and direct quote verified verbatim against the correct snapshot. Approved for publication as-is.
editor_notes.medical_review: Clinical/medical article — Rule 9 scrutiny applied. Each clinical statistic was checked against the specific snapshot it is attributed to. The strict requirement that no number rest on the 403/inaccessible Lancet paper is met: the article relies solely on the two Lilly press releases and BioSpace, all of which were retrieved at status 200 and read in full.
